channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Is Kaposi's sarcoma the end of the OX40/OX40L axis in atopic dermatitis?
item.description: The OX40/OX40L axis has shown durable activity in clinical development for atopic dermatitis with strong biological rationale, but questions have emerged regarding whether monotherapy benefits are sufficient compared to established and emerging treatment options. The development of this therapeutic

item.title: Systematic optimization of PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 CAR-T designs identifies a bicistronic dual-target, double-CD3ζ architecture with enhanced antitumor activity in gastric cancer
item.description: Researchers systematically optimized chimeric antigen receptor T-cell (CAR-T) designs targeting PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 to identify an enhanced architecture using a bicistronic construct with dual signaling domains (double-CD3ζ) for treating gastric cancer. This optimized dual-target, double-CD3ζ CAR-T d

item.title: EpiMII: Structure-Aware Graph Neural Networks for MHC-II Epitope Generation
item.description: EpiMII is a computational tool utilizing structure-aware graph neural networks to predict and generate major histocompatibility complex class II (MHC-II) epitopes. The approach integrates structural information through graph-based machine learning to improve epitope identification, supporting immuno

item.title: Advances in microneedle design for the delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells in the treatment of hypertrophic scars
item.description: This review examines advances in microneedle design for transdermal and intradermal delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells as therapeutic approaches for hypertrophic scar treatment. Microneedle technology offers minimally invasive delivery mechanisms that can enhance drug penetration and localized t

item.title: Fulcrumsec targets novo nordisk, leaks clinical and research data
item.description: FulcrumSec leaked data stolen from Novo Nordisk, claiming to have exfiltrated 1.3TB, including clinical records and AI research assets. On June 15, 2026, a data-theft extortion group calling itself FulcrumSec began leaking files from Novo Nordisk, the Danish …

item.title: Do we want to lead or lag in life sciences?
item.description: Life sciences leadership depends on strong ecosystems that connect science, capital and scale. Europe must do far more to support and incentivize innovation

item.title: Cygnvs launches command center for crises caused by a company’s own AI
item.description: Cyber resilience company CYGNVS Inc. today launched its AI Incident Command Center, a platform built to help organizations manage operational crises caused by their own artificial intelligence deployments. The product extends CYGNVS’ existing out-of-band inci…

item.title: 2026 top trends in academic libraries: a review of the trends and issues | planning and review committee | college & research libraries news
item.description: 2026 Top Trends in Academic Libraries: A Review of the Trends and Issues

item.title: Guideai health corp. announces closing of business combination, subscription receipt offering, and filing of final prospectus
item.description: Not for distribution to U.S. Newswire services or for dissemination in the United States. Any failure to comply with this restriction may constitute a violation of U.S. Securities laws. BOSTON, June 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — GuideAI Health Corp. (formerly, …

item.title: Towards autonomous medical artificial intelligence agents
item.description: A large language model artificial intelligence agent operating in a sandboxed electronic health record system can autonomously take patient histories, order tests, interpret findings, diagnose conditions and propose treatments, outperforming experienced …

item.title: “digital colonialism”: u.s. demands to access africans’ data raise privacy, sovereignty concerns
item.description: The United States is requiring access to health data as part of lifesaving aid deals with African countries. The U.S. says the data will be aggregated and anonymized, but privacy experts fear the information could be misused or exploited.

item.title: Night owls have 79% higher risk of poor heart health and 16% higher risk of heart attack, per 300,000-person study
item.description: A 300,000-person study in JAHA found night owls had 79% higher risk of poor heart health and 16% higher heart attack and stroke risk. Here's what it means and what to do.

item.title: Analysis of 173,303 exomes and genomes in the pakistan genome resource
item.description: The Pakistan Genome Resource compiles biobank data from 173,303 individuals with high familial relatedness, broadening the catalogue of human genetic variation and establishing a population-specific genomic reference for Pakistan.

item.title: System76 boss reckons he can liberate the entire pc stack... just give him another 15 years
item.description: Bootstrapped Linux box-botherer flogs new Thelio kit, talks up COSMIC, and politely declines to bolt AI onto everything

item.title: FDA Approves First Single-Dose Generic Treatment for Influenza
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved the first generic of Xofluza (baloxavir marboxil) tablets, the first single-dose treatment for acute uncomplicated influenza and prophylaxis in patients 5 years of age and older.

item.title: STAT+: FDA appears open to Moderna’s flu vaccine ahead of adcomm
item.description: UniQure will submit Huntington's therapy for approval, Eli Lilly buys 4E Therapeutics, and more biotech news

item.title: STAT+: Following dispute with FDA, UniQure is cleared to submit Huntington’s treatment for approval
item.description: The FDA has reversed its opposition to a closely watched experimental treatment for Huntington’s disease, clearing a path for its maker.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck on AI IPOs, personal agents, and the ROI reckoning
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses

item.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
item.description: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

item.title: A Medicinal Chemistry Perspective on Anticonvulsant Drug Design: Targets, Mechanisms, and Spiro Compounds
item.description: This review examines anticonvulsant drug design from a medicinal chemistry perspective, focusing on established drug targets, their mechanisms of action, and the role of spiro compounds as a structural class in anticonvulsant development. The analysis integrates current understanding of molecular ta

item.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to seal 2FA code from users
item.description: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

item.title: Autonomous labs are running science experiments 24/7
item.description: Robots and AI are running experiments around the clock, from battery chemistry to cancer therapies. But can they be trusted to get it right?

item.title: Kaiyi jiang
item.description: Creating AI platforms to discover new therapeutics

item.title: Antitrust’s new problem is that the future won’t stay still
item.description: Watch more: TechReg With Nitika Bagaria and Emily Chissell of Keystone In the artificial intelligence era, regulators are not just governing markets. They are governing uncertainty itself. As the technology reshapes industries from software and healthcare to …

item.title: Chris boshoff
item.description: The Pfizer executive talks about rekindling the public’s excitement over science

item.title: James manyika
item.description: The Google senior vice president talks about the need to reset the relationship between the government and academic and industrial science

item.title: Darío gil
item.description: The Department of Energy official discusses what will drive tomorrow’s innovation

item.title: Aviv regev
item.description: The biotech executive talks about how we are currently at an inflection point in biomedical science and how frustration can be a good thing

item.title: Canadian companies can't afford to wait for homegrown AI
item.description: Neil Cawse: Federal strategy is welcome, but rather than owning every server, we should be more concerned about controlling the data that runs on them

item.title: Mit study warns of major AI risk. is governance keeping up?
item.description: AI experts warn of catastrophic AI risks, but governance lags, with security concerns driving policy while broader risks aren't being fully addressed.

item.title: Stop treating AI as the strategy
item.description: AI delivers lasting value when strategy, governance and trust shape adoption.

item.title: Inside the fight over claude mythos 5
item.description: As the rest of the country celebrated the USA's first World Cup win and the New York Knicks championship, Anthropic spent its weekend fighting the Trump administration over its latest model release. At 5:21 PM on Friday, the company received a US export contr…

item.title: Introducing lakehouse//rt: real-time performance on a unified lakehouse
item.description: * Databricks launches Lakehouse//RT, powered by new real-time engine Reyden, to provide millisecond query speeds directly on your lakehouse without data movement * Preview users have seen up to 16x better performance vs. real-time serving layers, with respon…

item.title: Data strategy: why collecting more isn’t the answer
item.description: In her latest column, Charlotte McEleny looks at the APAC market, and why data accumulation is all for nothing if it’s not backed by sophisticated strategy… The post Data Strategy: Why Collecting More isn’t the Answer appeared first on ExchangeWire.com.

item.title: Rhythm pharmaceuticals announces multiple new data presentations from mc4r agonists in acquired hypothalamic obesity (ho), bardet-biedl syndrome (bbs) and prader-willi syndrome (pws) at endo 2026
item.description: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals Announces Multiple New Data Presentations from MC4R Agonists in Acquired Hypothalamic Obesity (HO), Bardet-Biedl Syndrome (BBS) and Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) at ENDO 2026biospace.com

item.title: Glp-1 drugs like ozempic are showing a 47 percent reduction in breast cancer risk in a major new study — and weight loss may not explain it
item.description: A study presented at ASCO 2026 and published June 10, 2026 found that GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, tirzepatide) are associated with a 30–47% reduction in breast cancer risk among women with diabetes or obesity. The effect may operate through mechanis…

item.title: Chatgpt's market share slips below 50% for first time | techcrunch
item.description: The chatbot still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide with over 1.1 billion monthly users, followed by Gemini with 662 million and Claude with 245 million.

item.title: Doj lawyers argue xai is ‘vital’ for national security in naacp lawsuit
item.description: In a bid to dismiss a lawsuit over xAI's polluting gas turbines, the Justice Department claimed the company is integral to military operations—including the Iran War.

item.title: Anthropic is still at odds with the white house over claude fable 5
item.description: Anthropic leaders flew to Washington, DC, to meet with White House officials on Monday. After high-level talks, they're still split on the risk Claude Fable 5 presents.

item.title: Sql server may be too lucrative for microsoft to ditch, but too legacy to love
item.description: Analysts say Redmond still has billions of reasons to keep backing its flagship DBMS, even as Azure, Postgres, and AI hog the spotlight

item.title: Erp users may soon get ahead by going headless, says rimini street boss
item.description: Look to AI agents and open source to escape the vendor-driven upgrade cycle

item.title: Inside the cloud's new agentic ai-ready, arm-powered foundation
item.description: PARTNER CONTENT: From hyperscalers to enterprises, performance-per-watt and system-level efficiency are redefining the cloud compute foundation

item.title: FDA Broadens Access to Over-the-Counter Naloxone Nasal Spray for Opioid Overdose
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) today approved another over-the-counter (OTC) intranasal naloxone product, Rextovy, a 4 milligram (mg) naloxone hydrochloride nasal spray for the emergency treatment of opioid overdose.

item.title: STAT+: How a biotech turned a trial failure into an AI model
item.description: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: How a biotech startup turned a failed clinical trial into an AI model, and more.

item.title: STAT+: ARCH-launched Neumora stops depression program
item.description: A new AI model to improve trial enrollment, importing drugs from Canada, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Verge Labs’ new AI model solves patient stratification problems for neurology clinical trials
item.description: As the saying goes, one man’s trash is another man’s treasure. Or as Verge Labs might put it, one company’s failed clinical trial … is that same company’s new AI…

item.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
item.description: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

item.title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
item.description: The chatbot still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide with over 1.1 billion monthly users, followed by Gemini with 662 million and Claude with 245 million.

item.title: Trump admin tries to block Clean Air Act lawsuit over xAI's gas turbines
item.description: NAACP lawsuit says xAI uses gas turbines without permits for Grok data center.
